1265→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1266→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1267→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1268→         */
1269→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1270→        /**
1271→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1272→         */
1273→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1274→    ) {
1275→        val nodes: String
1276→            get() =
1277→                fromASTNode
1278→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1279→                    ?.leaves()
1280→                    .orEmpty()
1281→                    .takeWhile {
1282→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1283→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1284→                    }
1285→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1286→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1287→
1288→        fun indent() =
1289→            if (activated) {
1290→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1291→            } else {
1292→                nodeIndent
1293→            }
1294→
1295→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1296→    }
1297→}
1298→
1299→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1300→
1301→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1302→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1303→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1304→            Pair("[", "]")
1305→        } else {
1306→            Pair("", "")
1307→        }
1308→    return prefix
1309→        .plus(
1310→            this
1311→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1312→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1313→        ).plus(suffix)
1314→}
1315→
1316→private class StringTemplateIndenter(
1317→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
1318→    private val indentConfig: IndentConfig,
1319→) {
1320→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1321→        expectedIndent: String,
1322→        node: ASTNode,
1323→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1324→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1325→    ) {
1326→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1327→        node
1328→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1329→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1330→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1331→            ?.let { _ ->
1332→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1333→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1334→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1335→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1336→                    emit(
1337→                        node.startOffset,
1338→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1339→                        false,
1340→                    )
1341→                    return
1342→                }
1343→
1344→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1345→                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
1346→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1347→                    if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()) {
1348→                        // Allow:
1349→                        //   fun foo(): String {
1350→                        //       return """
1351→                        //           some text
1352→                        //           """.trimIndent
1353→                        //   }
1354→                        node
1355→                            .indent(false)
1356→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1357→                    } else if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression()) {
1358→                        // Allow:
1359→                        //   fun foo(
1360→                        //       bar: String
1361→                        //   ) = """
1362→                        //       $bar
1363→                        //       """.trimIndent
1364→                        node
1365→                            .indent(false)
1366→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1367→                    } else if (prevLeaf?.text == "\n") {
1368→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1369→                        // should have no indent as well.
1370→                        ""
1371→                    } else {
1372→                        expectedIndent
1373→                    }
1374→                node.children()
1375→                    .forEach {
1376→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1377→                            (
1378→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1379→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1380→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1381→                            )
1382→                        ) {
1383→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1384→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1385→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1386→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1387→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1388→                                } else {
1389→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1390→                                }
1391→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1392→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1393→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1394→                                emit(
1395→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1396→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1397→                                        "string",
1398→                                    true,
1399→                                )
1400→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1401→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1402→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1403→                                    )
1404→                                }
1405→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1406→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1407→                                // the closing quotes.
1408→                                emit(
1409→                                    it.startOffset,
1410→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1411→                                    true,
1412→                                )
1413→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1414→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1415→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1416→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1417→                                        )
1418→                                    } else {
1419→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1420→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1421→                                        )
1422→                                    }
1423→                                }
1424→                            }
1425→                        }
1426→                    }
1427→            }
1428→    }
1429→
1430→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression() =
1431→        (prevLeaf()?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevLeaf()?.text == " ") &&
1432→            FUN ==
1433→            prevCodeLeaf()
1434→                .takeIf { it?.elementType == EQ }
1435→                ?.treeParent
1436→                ?.elementType
1437→
1438→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock() = RETURN_KEYWORD == prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType
1439→
1440→    /**
1441→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1442→     * the closing quotes.
1443→     */
1444→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1445→        children()
1446→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1447→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1448→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1449→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1450→            .let { indents ->
1451→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote =
1452→                    indents
1453→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1454→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1455→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1456→                } else {
1457→                    indents
1458→                }
1459→            }
1460→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1461→            .minOrNull()
1462→            ?: 0
1463→
1464→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
1465→
1466→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
1467→
1468→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1469→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1470→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
1471→
1472→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1473→        for (child in node.children()) {
1474→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1475→                val v = child.text
1476→                if (v == "\n") {
1477→                    return true
1478→                }
1479→            }
1480→        }
1481→        return false
1482→    }
1483→
1484→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1485→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1486→        val nonBlankLines =
1487→            this
1488→                .text
1489→                .split("\n")
1490→                .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1491→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1492→                .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1493→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1494→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1495→            nonBlankLines
1496→                .joinToString(separator = "") {
1497→                    it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1498→                }
1499→                .toCharArray()
1500→                .distinct()
1501→                .count()
1502→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1503→    }
1504→
1505→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1506→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1507→
1508→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1509→
1510→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =
1511→        elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY || elementType == SHORT_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
1512→
1513→    private fun ASTNode.isClosingQuote() = elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE
1514→
1515→    private fun String.indentLength() = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let { if (it == -1) length else it }
1516→
1517→    /**
1518→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1519→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1520→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1521→     * split index.
1522→     */
1523→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1524→        assert(index >= 0)
1525→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex =
1526→            indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1527→                if (it == -1) {
1528→                    this.length
1529→                } else {
1530→                    it
1531→                }
1532→            }
1533→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1534→        return Pair(
1535→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1536→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1537→        )
1538→    }
1539→
1540→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {
1541→        val firstLeafOnLine = prevLeaf { it.text == "\n" }
1542→        return if (firstLeafOnLine == null) {
1543→            this
1544→        } else {
1545→            firstLeafOnLine.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true) ?: this
1546→        }
1547→    }
1548→
1549→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine(): Boolean {
1550→        var node: ASTNode? = getFirstElementOnSameLine()
1551→        while (node != null && node != this && node.text.isWhitespace()) {
1552→            node = node.nextLeaf()
1553→        }
1554→        return node != this
1555→    }
1556→
1557→    private fun String.isWhitespace() = none { !it.isWhitespace() }
1558→}
1559→
1560→public val INDENTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = IndentationRule().ruleId

250→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED ->
251→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
252→                    // Contrary to the IntelliJ IDEA default formatter, do not indent the closing RPAR
253→                    startIndentContext(
254→                        fromAstNode = node,
255→                        lastChildIndent = "",
256→                    )
257→                } else if (node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF) {
258→                    startIndentContext(node)
259→                }
260→
261→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
262→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
263→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
264→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
265→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
266→                startIndentContext(node)
267→
268→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
269→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
270→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
271→                startIndentContext(
272→                    fromAstNode = node,
273→                    childIndent = "",
274→                )
275→
276→            node.elementType == IF ->
277→                visitIf(node)
278→
279→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
280→                visitLbrace(node)
281→
282→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
283→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
284→                startIndentContext(
285→                    fromAstNode = node,
286→                    lastChildIndent = "",
287→                )
288→
289→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
290→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
291→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
292→
293→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
294→                visitValueParameter(node)
295→
296→            node.elementType == FUN ->
297→                visitFun(node)
298→
299→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
300→                visitClass(node)
301→
302→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
303→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
304→
305→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
306→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
307→
308→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
309→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
310→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
311→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
312→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
313→                ) {
314→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
315→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
316→                    //    .bar[0] {
317→                    //        "foobar"
318→                    //    }
319→                    startIndentContext(
320→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
321→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
322→                    )
323→                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
324→                    startIndentContext(node)
325→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
326→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
327→                } else {
328→                    startIndentContext(node)
329→                }
330→            }
331→
332→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
333→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
334→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
335→
336→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
337→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
338→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
339→
340→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
341→                visitWhen(node)
342→
343→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
344→                visitWhenEntry(node)
345→
346→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
347→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
348→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)

131→public class IndentationRule :
132→    StandardRule(
133→        id = "indent",
134→        visitorModifiers =
135→            setOf(
136→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
137→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
138→                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
139→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
140→                ),
141→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
142→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
143→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
144→                ),
145→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
146→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
147→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
148→                ),
149→            ),
150→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
151→            setOf(
152→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
153→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
154→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
155→            ),
156→    ) {
157→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
158→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
159→
160→    private var line = 1
161→
162→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
163→
164→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
165→
166→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
167→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
168→        indentConfig =
169→            IndentConfig(
170→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
171→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
172→            )
173→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
174→            stopTraversalOfAST()
175→        }
176→    }
177→
178→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
179→        node: ASTNode,
180→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
181→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
182→    ) {
183→        if (node.isRoot()) {
184→            // File should not start with a whitespace
185→            node
186→                .nextLeaf()
187→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
188→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
189→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
190→                    if (autoCorrect) {
191→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
192→                    }
193→                }
194→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
195→        }
196→
197→        when {
198→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
199→                line++
200→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
201→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
202→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
203→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
204→                    )
205→                }
206→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
207→            }
208→
209→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
210→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
211→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
212→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
213→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
214→                startIndentContext(
215→                    fromAstNode = node,
216→                    lastChildIndent = "",
217→                )
218→
219→            node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY -> {
220→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfClassWithAMultilinePrimaryConstructor()) {
221→                    // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, indent the super type call entry so that it looks better in case it
222→                    // is followed by another super type:
223→                    //      class Foo(
224→                    //          val bar1: Bar,
225→                    //          val bar2: Bar,
226→                    //      ) : FooBar(
227→                    //              bar1,
228→                    //              bar2
229→                    //          ),
230→                    //          BarFoo,
231→                    startIndentContext(
232→                        fromAstNode = node,
233→                        childIndent = indentConfig.indent,
234→                        activated = true,
235→                    )
236→                } else {
237→                    startIndentContext(
238→                        fromAstNode = node,
239→                        lastChildIndent = "",
240→                    )
241→                }
242→            }
243→
244→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
245→                visitValueArgument(node)
246→
247→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
248→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
249→
250→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED ->
251→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
252→                    // Contrary to the IntelliJ IDEA default formatter, do not indent the closing RPAR
253→                    startIndentContext(
254→                        fromAstNode = node,
255→                        lastChildIndent = "",
256→                    )
257→                } else if (node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF) {
258→                    startIndentContext(node)
259→                }
260→
261→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
262→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
263→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
264→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
265→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
266→                startIndentContext(node)
267→
268→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
269→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
270→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
271→                startIndentContext(
272→                    fromAstNode = node,
273→                    childIndent = "",
274→                )
275→
276→            node.elementType == IF ->
277→                visitIf(node)
278→
279→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
280→                visitLbrace(node)
281→
282→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
283→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
284→                startIndentContext(
285→                    fromAstNode = node,
286→                    lastChildIndent = "",
287→                )
288→
289→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
290→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
291→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
292→
293→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
294→                visitValueParameter(node)
295→
296→            node.elementType == FUN ->
297→                visitFun(node)
298→
299→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
300→                visitClass(node)

conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→
 13→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 14→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 15→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
 16→* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
 17→* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
 18→* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
 19→* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
 20→
 21→### Changed
 22→
 23→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 24→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 25→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 26→
 27→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 28→
 29→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 30→
 31→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 32→
 33→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 34→
 35→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 36→
 37→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 38→
 39→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 40→
 41→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 42→
 43→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 44→
 45→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 46→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 47→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 48→
 49→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 50→
 51→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 52→
 53→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 54→
 55→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 56→
 57→### Added
 58→
 59→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 60→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 61→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 62→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 63→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 64→* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 65→* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 66→* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 67→
 68→### Removed
 69→
 70→* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 71→
 72→### Fixed
 73→
 74→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 75→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 76→* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 77→* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 78→* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 79→* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 80→
 81→### Changed
 82→
 83→* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 84→* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 85→* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 86→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 87→* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 88→* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 89→* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 90→
 91→## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 92→
 93→### Added
 94→
 95→### Removed
 96→
 97→### Fixed
 98→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 99→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
100→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))

  71→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TRY
  72→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPEALIAS
  73→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  74→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_CONSTRAINT
  75→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST
  76→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST
  77→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
  78→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.USER_TYPE
  79→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT
  80→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  81→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
  82→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
  83→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHEN
  84→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHEN_ENTRY
  85→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHERE_KEYWORD
  86→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHILE
  87→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  88→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
  89→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  90→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  91→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
  92→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  93→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
  94→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
  95→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue
  96→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
  97→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  98→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  99→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 100→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 101→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 102→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOf
 103→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 104→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isRoot
 105→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 106→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 107→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline
 108→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 109→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 110→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeSibling
 111→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 112→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 113→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.parent
 114→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 115→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeSibling
 116→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 117→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling

 119→import mu.KotlinLogging
 120→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 121→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiComment
 122→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 123→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtStringTemplateExpression

 125→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.parents

 128→
 129→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 130→
 131→public class IndentationRule :
 132→    StandardRule(
 133→        id = "indent",
 134→        visitorModifiers =
 135→            setOf(
 136→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 137→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 138→                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 139→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 140→                ),
 141→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 142→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 143→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 144→                ),
 145→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 146→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 147→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 148→                ),
 149→            ),
 150→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 151→            setOf(
 152→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 153→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 154→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 155→            ),
 156→    ) {
 157→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 158→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 159→
 160→    private var line = 1
 161→
 162→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 163→
 164→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 165→
 166→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 167→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 168→        indentConfig =
 169→            IndentConfig(
 170→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 171→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 172→            )
 173→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 174→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 175→        }
 176→    }
 177→
 178→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 179→        node: ASTNode,
 180→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 181→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 182→    ) {
 183→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 184→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 185→            node
 186→                .nextLeaf()
 187→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 188→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 189→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 190→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 191→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 192→                    }
 193→                }
 194→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 195→        }
 196→
 197→        when {
 198→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 199→                line++
 200→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 201→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 202→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 203→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 204→                    )
 205→                }
 206→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 207→            }
 208→
 209→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 210→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 211→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 212→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 213→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 214→                startIndentContext(
 215→                    fromAstNode = node,
 216→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 217→                )
 218→
 219→            node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY -> {
 220→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfClassWithAMultilinePrimaryConstructor()) {
 221→                    // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, indent the super type call entry so that it looks better in case it
 222→                    // is followed by another super type:
 223→                    //      class Foo(
 224→                    //          val bar1: Bar,
 225→                    //          val bar2: Bar,
 226→                    //      ) : FooBar(
 227→                    //              bar1,
 228→                    //              bar2
 229→                    //          ),
 230→                    //          BarFoo,
 231→                    startIndentContext(
 232→                        fromAstNode = node,
 233→                        childIndent = indentConfig.indent,
 234→                        activated = true,
 235→                    )
 236→                } else {
 237→                    startIndentContext(
 238→                        fromAstNode = node,
 239→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 240→                    )
 241→                }
 242→            }
 243→
 244→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 245→                visitValueArgument(node)
 246→
 247→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 248→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 249→
 250→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED ->
 251→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 252→                    // Contrary to the IntelliJ IDEA default formatter, do not indent the closing RPAR
 253→                    startIndentContext(
 254→                        fromAstNode = node,
 255→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 256→                    )
 257→                } else if (node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF) {
 258→                    startIndentContext(node)
 259→                }
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 262→                node.elementType == IS_EXPRESSION ||
 263→                node.elementType == PREFIX_EXPRESSION ||
 264→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 265→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 266→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 267→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 268→                startIndentContext(node)
 269→
 270→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 271→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 272→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 273→                startIndentContext(
 274→                    fromAstNode = node,
 275→                    childIndent = "",
 276→                )
 277→
 278→            node.elementType == IF ->
 279→                visitIf(node)
 280→
 281→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 282→                visitLbrace(node)
 283→
 284→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 285→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 286→                startIndentContext(
 287→                    fromAstNode = node,
 288→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 289→                )
 290→
 291→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 292→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 293→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 294→
 295→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 296→                visitValueParameter(node)
 297→
 298→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 299→                visitFun(node)
 300→
 301→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 302→                visitClass(node)
 303→
 304→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 305→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 306→
 307→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 308→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 309→
 310→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 311→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 312→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 313→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 314→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 315→                ) {
 316→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 317→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 318→                    //    .bar[0] {
 319→                    //        "foobar"
 320→                    //    }
 321→                    startIndentContext(
 322→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 323→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 324→                    )
 325→                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 326→                    startIndentContext(node)
 327→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 328→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 329→                } else {
 330→                    startIndentContext(node)
 331→                }
 332→            }
 333→
 334→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 335→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 336→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 337→
 338→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 339→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 340→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 341→
 342→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 343→                visitWhen(node)
 344→
 345→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 346→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 347→
 348→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 349→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 350→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 351→
 352→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 353→                visitKdoc(node)
 354→
 355→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 356→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 357→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 358→
 359→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 360→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 361→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 362→
 363→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 364→                visitLBracket(node)
 365→
 366→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 367→                visitNullableType(node)
 368→
 369→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 370→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 371→
 372→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 373→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 374→
 375→            else -> {
 376→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 377→            }
 378→        }
 379→    }
 380→
 381→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfClassWithAMultilinePrimaryConstructor() =
 382→        parent { it.elementType == CLASS }
 383→            ?.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 384→            ?.textContains('\n') == true
 385→
 386→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 387→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 388→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 389→            //     val foo = foo(
 390→            //         parameterName =
 391→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 392→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 393→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 394→            //     )
 395→            startIndentContext(
 396→                fromAstNode = node,
 397→                lastChildIndent = "",
 398→            )
 399→        }
 400→    }
 401→
 402→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 403→        node
 404→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 405→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 406→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 407→                val nextToAstNode =
 408→                    startIndentContext(
 409→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 410→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 411→
 412→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 413→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 414→                    startIndentContext(
 415→                        fromAstNode = node,
 416→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 417→                        childIndent = "",
 418→                    )
 419→                }
 420→            }
 421→    }
 422→
 423→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 424→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 425→        node
 426→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 427→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 428→                nextToAstNode =
 429→                    startIndentContext(
 430→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 431→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 432→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 433→            }
 434→
 435→        node
 436→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 437→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 438→            ?.nextLeaf()
 439→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 440→                nextToAstNode =
 441→                    startIndentContext(
 442→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 443→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 444→                        childIndent = "",
 445→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 446→            }
 447→        node
 448→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 449→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 450→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 451→                nextToAstNode =
 452→                    startIndentContext(
 453→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 454→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 455→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 456→            }
 457→        startIndentContext(
 458→            fromAstNode = node,
 459→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 460→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 461→        )
 462→    }
 463→
 464→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 465→        // Outer indent context
 466→        val rbrace =
 467→            requireNotNull(
 468→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 469→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 470→        startIndentContext(
 471→            fromAstNode = node,
 472→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 473→            firstChildIndent = "",
 474→            lastChildIndent = "",
 475→        )
 476→
 477→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 478→        node
 479→            .treeParent
 480→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 481→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 482→            ?.let { arrow ->
 483→                startIndentContext(
 484→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 485→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 486→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 487→                )
 488→                startIndentContext(
 489→                    fromAstNode = node,
 490→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 491→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 492→                )
 493→            }
 494→    }
 495→
 496→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 497→        if (isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 498→            // val fieldExample =
 499→            //      LongNameClass {
 500→            //              paramA,
 501→            //              paramB,
 502→            //              paramC ->
 503→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 504→            //      }
 505→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 506→        } else {
 507→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 508→            // val fieldExample =
 509→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 510→            //                     paramB,
 511→            //                     paramC ->
 512→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 513→            //     }
 514→            // val fieldExample =
 515→            //     someFunction(
 516→            //         1,
 517→            //         2,
 518→            //     ) { paramA,
 519→            //         paramB,
 520→            //         paramC ->
 521→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 522→            //     }
 523→            this
 524→                .takeIf { it.isPartOf(CALL_EXPRESSION) }
 525→                ?.treeParent
 526→                ?.leaves(false)
 527→                ?.takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 528→                ?.sumOf { it.textLength }
 529→                ?.plus(2) // need to add spaces to compensate for "{ "
 530→                ?.let { length -> " ".repeat(length) }
 531→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 532→        }
 533→
 534→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 535→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 536→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 537→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 538→
 539→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 540→        startIndentContext(
 541→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 542→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 543→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 544→            childIndent = "",
 545→        )
 546→    }
 547→
 548→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 549→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 550→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 551→        node
 552→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 553→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 554→                nextToAstNode =
 555→                    startIndentContext(
 556→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 557→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 558→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 559→            }
 560→
 561→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 562→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 563→            //     fun process(
 564→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 565→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 566→            //     ): List<Output>
 567→            node
 568→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 569→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 570→                    nextToAstNode =
 571→                        startIndentContext(
 572→                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 573→                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 574→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 575→                }
 576→        }
 577→
 578→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 579→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 580→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 581→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 582→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 583→        ) {
 584→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 585→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 586→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 587→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 588→        } else {
 589→            startIndentContext(
 590→                fromAstNode = node,
 591→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 592→                childIndent = "",
 593→            )
 594→        }
 595→    }
 596→
 597→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 598→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 599→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 600→        val eqOrBlock =
 601→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 602→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 603→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 604→            nextToAstNode =
 605→                startIndentContext(
 606→                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 607→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 608→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 609→        }
 610→
 611→        node
 612→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 613→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 614→                nextToAstNode =
 615→                    startIndentContext(
 616→                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 617→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 618→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 619→            }
 620→
 621→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 622→        startIndentContext(
 623→            fromAstNode = node,
 624→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 625→            childIndent = "",
 626→        )
 627→    }
 628→
 629→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 630→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 631→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 632→        node
 633→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 634→            ?.let { where ->
 635→                val typeConstraintList =
 636→                    requireNotNull(
 637→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 638→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 639→                require(
 640→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 641→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 642→                nextToAstNode =
 643→                    startIndentContext(
 644→                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 645→                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 646→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 647→            }
 648→
 649→        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 650→        val containsConstructorKeyword = primaryConstructor?.findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD) != null
 651→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null && containsConstructorKeyword) {
 652→            // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, ident both constructor and super type list as follows:
 653→            //     class Foo
 654→            //        @Bar1 @Bar2
 655→            //        constructor(
 656→            //            foo1: Foo1,
 657→            //            foo2: Foo2,
 658→            //        ) : Foobar1(
 659→            //                "foobar1",
 660→            //                "foobar2",
 661→            //            ),
 662→            //            FooBar2,
 663→            val superTypeList = node.findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 664→            nextToAstNode =
 665→                startIndentContext(
 666→                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 667→                    toAstNode =
 668→                        superTypeList
 669→                            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 670→                            ?: nextToAstNode,
 671→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 672→
 673→            superTypeList
 674→                ?.findChildByType(COMMA)
 675→                ?.let { comma ->
 676→                    // In case of a multiline primary constructor the first super type is merged with the closing parenthesis of the
 677→                    // constructor. The start of the super type list does not activate the indent because it is not preceded by a newline.
 678→                    // To fix this, the super type entries on the next line (e.g. after the comma) have to be double indented.
 679→                    // Allow:
 680→                    //     class Foo(
 681→                    //         val bar1: Bar,
 682→                    //         val bar2: Bar,
 683→                    //     ) : FooBar(
 684→                    //             bar1,
 685→                    //             bar2
 686→                    //         ),
 687→                    //         BarFoo1
 688→                    val prevCodeLeaf =
 689→                        startIndentContext(
 690→                            fromAstNode = comma,
 691→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 692→                            childIndent = indentConfig.indent.repeat(2),
 693→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 694→                    startIndentContext(
 695→                        fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 696→                        toAstNode = prevCodeLeaf,
 697→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 698→                }
 699→        } else {
 700→            node
 701→                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 702→                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 703→                    nextToAstNode =
 704→                        startIndentContext(
 705→                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 706→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 707→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 708→                }
 709→        }
 710→
 711→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 712→        startIndentContext(
 713→            fromAstNode = node,
 714→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 715→            childIndent = "",
 716→        )
 717→    }

 740→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 741→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 742→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 743→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 744→            val conditionIndentContext =
 745→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 746→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 747→            startIndentContext(
 748→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 749→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 750→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 751→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 752→            )
 753→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 754→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 755→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 756→        ) {
 757→            startIndentContext(node)
 758→        }
 759→    }

 961→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 962→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 963→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 964→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 965→                modifierList
 966→                    .children()
 967→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 968→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 969→            }
 970→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 971→            ?: this
 972→    }

 974→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 975→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 976→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 977→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 978→        ) {
 979→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 980→        }
 981→        return fromAstNode!!
 982→    }

 984→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =

 986→            .parents()
 987→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 988→            .lastOrNull()
 989→            ?.elementType == CONDITION

 991→    private fun currentIndent() =
 992→        indentContextStack
 993→            .peekLast()
 994→            .indent()

 996→    private fun startIndentContext(
 997→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 998→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 999→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
1000→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
1001→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
1002→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
1003→        activated: Boolean = false,
1004→    ): IndentContext =
1005→        IndentContext(
1006→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
1007→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
1008→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
1009→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
1010→            childIndent = childIndent,
1011→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
1012→            activated = activated,
1013→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
1014→            LOGGER.trace {
1015→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
1016→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
1017→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
1018→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
1019→            }
1020→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
1021→        }

1023→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
1024→        node: ASTNode,
1025→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1026→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1027→    ) {
1028→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
1029→            LOGGER.trace {
1030→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1031→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
1032→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
1033→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
1034→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
1035→            }
1036→            indentContextStack
1037→                .removeLast()
1038→                .also {
1039→                    LOGGER.trace {
1040→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1041→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
1042→                        val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
1043→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
1044→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"
1045→                    }
1046→                }
1047→        }
1048→    }

1050→    override fun afterLastNode() {
1051→        require(indentContextStack.isEmpty()) {
1052→            indentContextStack
1053→                .joinToString(prefix = "Stack should be empty:\n\t", separator = "\n\t") { it.toString() }
1054→        }
1055→    }

1057→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
1058→        node: ASTNode,
1059→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1060→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1061→    ) {
1062→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
1063→            return
1064→        }
1065→
1066→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
1067→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
1068→        val text = node.text
1069→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1070→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
1071→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
1072→                emit(
1073→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1074→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
1075→                    true,
1076→                )
1077→            } else {
1078→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
1079→                // been emitted.
1080→            }
1081→            LOGGER.trace {
1082→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
1083→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
1084→            }
1085→            if (autoCorrect) {
1086→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1087→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
1088→                )
1089→            }
1090→        } else {
1091→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1092→        }
1093→    }
1094→
1095→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
1096→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1097→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
1098→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1099→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
1100→        }
1101→
1102→        nextLeaf
1103→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
1104→            ?.let { comment ->
1105→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
1106→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1107→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
1108→                }
1109→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
1110→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
1111→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
1112→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1113→                    return true
1114→                }
1115→            }
1116→        return false
1117→    }
1118→
1119→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""
1120→
1121→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =
1122→        LOGGER.trace { "No indentation change required for $elementType: ${textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
1123→
1124→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
1125→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1126→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1127→        val adjustedChildIndent =
1128→            when {
1129→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
1130→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1131→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1132→
1133→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1134→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1135→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1136→
1137→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1138→            }
1139→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1140→    }
1141→
1142→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1143→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1144→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1145→            SPACE -> {
1146→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1147→                    emit(
1148→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1149→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1150→                        true,
1151→                    )
1152→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1153→                } else {
1154→                    nodeIndent
1155→                }
1156→            }
1157→
1158→            TAB -> {
1159→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1160→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1161→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1162→                    emit(
1163→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1164→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1165→                        true,
1166→                    )
1167→                    indentConfig
1168→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1169→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1170→                } else {
1171→                    nodeIndent
1172→                }
1173→            }
1174→        }
1175→    }
1176→
1177→    private fun visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(
1178→        node: ASTNode,
1179→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1180→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1181→    ) {
1182→        if (!this::stringTemplateIndenter.isInitialized) {
1183→            stringTemplateIndenter = StringTemplateIndenter(codeStyle, indentConfig)
1184→        }
1185→        stringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes(currentIndent(), node.treeParent, autoCorrect, emit)
1186→    }

1188→    private fun ASTNode?.isElvisOperator() =
1189→        this != null &&
1190→            elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE &&
1191→            firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
