1193→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1194→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1195→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1196→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1197→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1198→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1199→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1200→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1201→                }
1202→
1203→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1204→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1205→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1206→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1207→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1208→                }
1209→
1210→                else -> ""
1211→            }
1212→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1213→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1214→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1215→        } else {
1216→            ""
1217→        }
1218→    }

1230→    private companion object {
1231→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1232→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1233→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION = setOf(DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION, SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION)
1234→    }

1236→    private data class IndentContext(
1237→        /**
1238→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1239→         */
1240→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1241→        /**
1242→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1243→         * on which the indent context starts.
1244→         */
1245→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1246→        /**
1247→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1248→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1249→         */
1250→        val nodeIndent: String,
1251→        /**
1252→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1253→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1254→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1255→         */
1256→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1257→        /**
1258→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1259→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1260→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1261→         */
1262→        val childIndent: String,
1263→        /**
1264→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1265→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1266→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1267→         */
1268→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1269→        /**
1270→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1271→         */
1272→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1273→    ) {
1274→        val nodes: String
1275→            get() =
1276→                fromASTNode
1277→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1278→                    ?.leaves()
1279→                    .orEmpty()
1280→                    .takeWhile {
1281→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1282→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1283→                    }
1284→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1285→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1286→
1287→        fun indent() =
1288→            if (activated) {
1289→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1290→            } else {
1291→                nodeIndent
1292→            }
1293→
1294→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1295→    }
1296→}
1297→
1298→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1299→
1300→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1301→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1302→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1303→            Pair("[", "]")
1304→        } else {
1305→            Pair("", "")
1306→        }
1307→    return prefix
1308→        .plus(
1309→            this
1310→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1311→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1312→        ).plus(suffix)
1313→}
1314→
1315→private class StringTemplateIndenter(
1316→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
1317→    private val indentConfig: IndentConfig,
1318→) {
1319→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1320→        expectedIndent: String,
1321→        node: ASTNode,
1322→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1323→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1324→    ) {
1325→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1326→        node
1327→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1328→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1329→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1330→            ?.let { _ ->
1331→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1332→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1333→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1334→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1335→                    emit(
1336→                        node.startOffset,
1337→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1338→                        false,
1339→                    )
1340→                    return
1341→                }
1342→
1343→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1344→                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
1345→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1346→                    if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()) {
1347→                        // Allow:
1348→                        //   fun foo(): String {
1349→                        //       return """
1350→                        //           some text
1351→                        //           """.trimIndent
1352→                        //   }
1353→                        node
1354→                            .indent(false)
1355→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1356→                    } else if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression()) {
1357→                        // Allow:
1358→                        //   fun foo(
1359→                        //       bar: String
1360→                        //   ) = """
1361→                        //       $bar
1362→                        //       """.trimIndent
1363→                        node
1364→                            .indent(false)
1365→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1366→                    } else if (prevLeaf?.text == "\n") {
1367→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1368→                        // should have no indent as well.
1369→                        ""
1370→                    } else {
1371→                        expectedIndent
1372→                    }
1373→                node.children()
1374→                    .forEach {
1375→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1376→                            (
1377→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1378→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1379→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1380→                            )
1381→                        ) {
1382→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1383→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1384→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1385→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1386→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1387→                                } else {
1388→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1389→                                }
1390→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1391→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1392→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1393→                                emit(
1394→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1395→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1396→                                        "string",
1397→                                    true,
1398→                                )
1399→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1400→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1401→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1402→                                    )
1403→                                }
1404→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1405→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1406→                                // the closing quotes.
1407→                                emit(
1408→                                    it.startOffset,
1409→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1410→                                    true,
1411→                                )
1412→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1413→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1414→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1415→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1416→                                        )
1417→                                    } else {
1418→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1419→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1420→                                        )
1421→                                    }
1422→                                }
1423→                            }
1424→                        }
1425→                    }
1426→            }
1427→    }
1428→
1429→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression() =
1430→        (prevLeaf()?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevLeaf()?.text == " ") &&
1431→            FUN ==
1432→            prevCodeLeaf()
1433→                .takeIf { it?.elementType == EQ }
1434→                ?.treeParent
1435→                ?.elementType
1436→
1437→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock() = RETURN_KEYWORD == prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType
1438→
1439→    /**
1440→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1441→     * the closing quotes.
1442→     */
1443→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1444→        children()
1445→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1446→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1447→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1448→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1449→            .let { indents ->
1450→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote =
1451→                    indents
1452→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1453→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1454→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1455→                } else {
1456→                    indents
1457→                }
1458→            }
1459→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1460→            .minOrNull()
1461→            ?: 0
1462→
1463→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
1464→
1465→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
1466→
1467→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1468→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1469→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
1470→
1471→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1472→        for (child in node.children()) {
1473→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1474→                val v = child.text
1475→                if (v == "\n") {
1476→                    return true
1477→                }
1478→            }
1479→        }
1480→        return false
1481→    }
1482→
1483→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1484→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1485→        val nonBlankLines =
1486→            this
1487→                .text
1488→                .split("\n")
1489→                .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1490→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1491→                .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1492→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1493→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1494→            nonBlankLines
1495→                .joinToString(separator = "") {
1496→                    it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1497→                }
1498→                .toCharArray()
1499→                .distinct()
1500→                .count()
1501→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1502→    }
1503→
1504→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1505→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1506→
1507→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1508→
1509→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =
1510→        elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY || elementType == SHORT_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
1511→
1512→    private fun ASTNode.isClosingQuote() = elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE
1513→
1514→    private fun String.indentLength() = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let { if (it == -1) length else it }
1515→
1516→    /**
1517→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1518→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1519→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1520→     * split index.
1521→     */
1522→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1523→        assert(index >= 0)
1524→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex =
1525→            indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1526→                if (it == -1) {
1527→                    this.length
1528→                } else {
1529→                    it
1530→                }
1531→            }
1532→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1533→        return Pair(
1534→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1535→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1536→        )
1537→    }
1538→
1539→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {
1540→        val firstLeafOnLine = prevLeaf { it.text == "\n" }
1541→        return if (firstLeafOnLine == null) {
1542→            this
1543→        } else {
1544→            firstLeafOnLine.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true) ?: this
1545→        }
1546→    }
1547→
1548→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine(): Boolean {
1549→        var node: ASTNode? = getFirstElementOnSameLine()
1550→        while (node != null && node != this && node.text.isWhitespace()) {
1551→            node = node.nextLeaf()
1552→        }
1553→        return node != this
1554→    }
1555→
1556→    private fun String.isWhitespace() = none { !it.isWhitespace() }
1557→}
1558→
1559→public val INDENTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = IndentationRule().ruleId

content.text:
The issue was that `IS_EXPRESSION` (e.g., `any is Foo`) and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` (e.g., `++i`) were not explicitly handled in `IndentationRule.kt`. As a result, when these expressions were split across multiple lines, the indentation of the second line (the right-hand side or operand) was not being indented correctly relative to the start of the expression.

I fixed this by:
1.  Importing `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` from `ElementType` in `IndentationRule.kt`.
2.  Adding these element types to the `when` clause in `beforeVisitChildNodes` to trigger `startIndentContext(node)`. This ensures that a new indentation context is created for these expressions, which correctly calculates the expected indentation for their children based on the parent's indentation.

I verified the fix by creating a new test file `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleIsExpressionTest.kt` with test cases reproducing the reported issue. The tests initially failed as expected, and passed after applying the fix.

I also updated `CHANGELOG.md` to document the fix.

**Modified Files:**
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`: Added `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` to handling logic.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: Added entry for the fix.

**Deleted Files:**
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleIsExpressionTest.kt`: Removed after verification.

 129→public class IndentationRule :
 130→    StandardRule(
 131→        id = "indent",
 132→        visitorModifiers =
 133→            setOf(
 134→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 135→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 136→                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 137→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 138→                ),
 139→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 140→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 141→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 142→                ),
 143→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 144→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 145→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 146→                ),
 147→            ),
 148→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 149→            setOf(
 150→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 151→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 152→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 153→            ),
 154→    ) {
 155→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 156→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 157→
 158→    private var line = 1
 159→
 160→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 161→
 162→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 163→
 164→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 165→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 166→        indentConfig =
 167→            IndentConfig(
 168→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 169→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 170→            )
 171→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 172→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 173→        }
 174→    }
 175→
 176→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 177→        node: ASTNode,
 178→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 179→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 180→    ) {
 181→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 182→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 183→            node
 184→                .nextLeaf()
 185→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 186→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 187→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 188→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 189→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 190→                    }
 191→                }
 192→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 193→        }
 194→
 195→        when {
 196→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 197→                line++
 198→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 199→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 200→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 201→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 202→                    )
 203→                }
 204→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 205→            }
 206→
 207→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 208→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 209→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 210→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 211→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 212→                startIndentContext(
 213→                    fromAstNode = node,
 214→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 215→                )
 216→
 217→            node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY -> {
 218→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfClassWithAMultilinePrimaryConstructor()) {
 219→                    // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, indent the super type call entry so that it looks better in case it
 220→                    // is followed by another super type:
 221→                    //      class Foo(
 222→                    //          val bar1: Bar,
 223→                    //          val bar2: Bar,
 224→                    //      ) : FooBar(
 225→                    //              bar1,
 226→                    //              bar2
 227→                    //          ),
 228→                    //          BarFoo,
 229→                    startIndentContext(
 230→                        fromAstNode = node,
 231→                        childIndent = indentConfig.indent,
 232→                        activated = true,
 233→                    )
 234→                } else {
 235→                    startIndentContext(
 236→                        fromAstNode = node,
 237→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 238→                    )
 239→                }
 240→            }
 241→
 242→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 243→                visitValueArgument(node)
 244→
 245→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 246→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 247→
 248→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED ->
 249→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 250→                    // Contrary to the IntelliJ IDEA default formatter, do not indent the closing RPAR
 251→                    startIndentContext(
 252→                        fromAstNode = node,
 253→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 254→                    )
 255→                } else if (node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF) {
 256→                    startIndentContext(node)
 257→                }
 258→
 259→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 260→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 261→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 262→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 263→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 264→                startIndentContext(node)
 265→
 266→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 267→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 268→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 269→                startIndentContext(
 270→                    fromAstNode = node,
 271→                    childIndent = "",
 272→                )
 273→
 274→            node.elementType == IF ->
 275→                visitIf(node)
 276→
 277→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 278→                visitLbrace(node)
 279→
 280→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 281→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 282→                startIndentContext(
 283→                    fromAstNode = node,
 284→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 285→                )
 286→
 287→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 288→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 289→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 290→
 291→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 292→                visitValueParameter(node)
 293→
 294→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 295→                visitFun(node)
 296→
 297→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 298→                visitClass(node)
 299→
 300→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 301→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 302→
 303→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 304→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 305→
 306→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 307→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 308→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 309→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 310→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 311→                ) {
 312→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 313→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 314→                    //    .bar[0] {
 315→                    //        "foobar"
 316→                    //    }
 317→                    startIndentContext(
 318→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 319→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 320→                    )
 321→                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 322→                    startIndentContext(node)
 323→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 324→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 325→                } else {
 326→                    startIndentContext(node)
 327→                }
 328→            }
 329→
 330→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 331→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 332→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 333→
 334→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 335→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 336→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 337→
 338→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 339→                visitWhen(node)
 340→
 341→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 342→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 343→
 344→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 345→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 346→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 347→
 348→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 349→                visitKdoc(node)
 350→
 351→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 352→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 353→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 354→
 355→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 356→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 357→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 358→
 359→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 360→                visitLBracket(node)
 361→
 362→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 363→                visitNullableType(node)
 364→
 365→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 366→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 367→
 368→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 369→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 370→
 371→            else -> {
 372→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 373→            }
 374→        }
 375→    }

 377→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfClassWithAMultilinePrimaryConstructor() =
 378→        parent { it.elementType == CLASS }
 379→            ?.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 380→            ?.textContains('\n') == true

 382→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 383→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 384→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 385→            //     val foo = foo(
 386→            //         parameterName =
 387→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 388→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 389→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 390→            //     )
 391→            startIndentContext(
 392→                fromAstNode = node,
 393→                lastChildIndent = "",
 394→            )
 395→        }
 396→    }

 398→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 399→        node
 400→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 401→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 402→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 403→                val nextToAstNode =
 404→                    startIndentContext(
 405→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 406→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 407→
 408→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 409→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 410→                    startIndentContext(
 411→                        fromAstNode = node,
 412→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 413→                        childIndent = "",
 414→                    )

 416→            }

 419→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 420→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 421→        node
 422→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 423→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 424→                nextToAstNode =
 425→                    startIndentContext(
 426→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 427→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 428→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 429→            }
 430→
 431→        node
 432→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 433→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 434→            ?.nextLeaf()
 435→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 436→                nextToAstNode =
 437→                    startIndentContext(
 438→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 439→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 440→                        childIndent = "",
 441→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 442→            }
 443→        node
 444→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 445→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 446→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 447→                nextToAstNode =
 448→                    startIndentContext(
 449→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 450→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 451→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 452→            }
 453→        startIndentContext(
 454→            fromAstNode = node,
 455→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 456→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 457→        )
 458→    }

 460→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 461→        // Outer indent context
 462→        val rbrace =
 463→            requireNotNull(
 464→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 465→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 466→        startIndentContext(
 467→            fromAstNode = node,
 468→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 469→            firstChildIndent = "",
 470→            lastChildIndent = "",
 471→        )
 472→
 473→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 474→        node
 475→            .treeParent
 476→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 477→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 478→            ?.let { arrow ->
 479→                startIndentContext(
 480→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 481→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 482→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 483→                )
 484→                startIndentContext(
 485→                    fromAstNode = node,
 486→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 487→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 488→                )
 489→            }
 490→    }
 491→
 492→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 493→        if (isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 494→            // val fieldExample =
 495→            //      LongNameClass {
 496→            //              paramA,
 497→            //              paramB,
 498→            //              paramC ->
 499→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 500→            //      }
 501→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 502→        } else {
 503→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 504→            // val fieldExample =
 505→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 506→            //                     paramB,
 507→            //                     paramC ->
 508→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 509→            //     }
 510→            // val fieldExample =
 511→            //     someFunction(
 512→            //         1,
 513→            //         2,
 514→            //     ) { paramA,
 515→            //         paramB,
 516→            //         paramC ->
 517→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 518→            //     }
 519→            this
 520→                .takeIf { it.isPartOf(CALL_EXPRESSION) }
 521→                ?.treeParent
 522→                ?.leaves(false)
 523→                ?.takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 524→                ?.sumOf { it.textLength }
 525→                ?.plus(2) // need to add spaces to compensate for "{ "
 526→                ?.let { length -> " ".repeat(length) }
 527→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 528→        }
 529→
 530→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 531→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 532→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 533→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 534→
 535→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 536→        startIndentContext(
 537→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 538→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 539→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 540→            childIndent = "",
 541→        )
 542→    }
 543→
 544→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 545→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 546→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 547→        node
 548→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 549→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 550→                nextToAstNode =
 551→                    startIndentContext(
 552→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 553→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 554→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 555→            }
 556→
 557→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 558→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 559→            //     fun process(
 560→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 561→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 562→            //     ): List<Output>
 563→            node
 564→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 565→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 566→                    nextToAstNode =
 567→                        startIndentContext(
 568→                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 569→                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 570→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 571→                }
 572→        }
 573→
 574→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 575→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 576→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 577→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 578→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 579→        ) {
 580→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 581→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 582→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 583→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 584→        } else {
 585→            startIndentContext(
 586→                fromAstNode = node,
 587→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 588→                childIndent = "",
 589→            )
 590→        }
 591→    }
 592→
 593→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 594→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 595→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 596→        val eqOrBlock =
 597→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 598→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 599→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 600→            nextToAstNode =
 601→                startIndentContext(
 602→                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 603→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 604→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 605→        }
 606→
 607→        node
 608→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 609→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 610→                nextToAstNode =
 611→                    startIndentContext(
 612→                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 613→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 614→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 615→            }
 616→
 617→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 618→        startIndentContext(
 619→            fromAstNode = node,
 620→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 621→            childIndent = "",
 622→        )
 623→    }
 624→
 625→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 626→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 627→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 628→        node
 629→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 630→            ?.let { where ->
 631→                val typeConstraintList =
 632→                    requireNotNull(
 633→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 634→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 635→                require(
 636→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 637→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 638→                nextToAstNode =
 639→                    startIndentContext(
 640→                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 641→                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 642→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 643→            }
 644→
 645→        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 646→        val containsConstructorKeyword = primaryConstructor?.findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD) != null
 647→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null && containsConstructorKeyword) {
 648→            // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, ident both constructor and super type list as follows:
 649→            //     class Foo
 650→            //        @Bar1 @Bar2
 651→            //        constructor(
 652→            //            foo1: Foo1,
 653→            //            foo2: Foo2,
 654→            //        ) : Foobar1(
 655→            //                "foobar1",
 656→            //                "foobar2",
 657→            //            ),
 658→            //            FooBar2,
 659→            val superTypeList = node.findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 660→            nextToAstNode =
 661→                startIndentContext(
 662→                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 663→                    toAstNode =
 664→                        superTypeList
 665→                            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 666→                            ?: nextToAstNode,
 667→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 668→
 669→            superTypeList
 670→                ?.findChildByType(COMMA)
 671→                ?.let { comma ->
 672→                    // In case of a multiline primary constructor the first super type is merged with the closing parenthesis of the
 673→                    // constructor. The start of the super type list does not activate the indent because it is not preceded by a newline.
 674→                    // To fix this, the super type entries on the next line (e.g. after the comma) have to be double indented.
 675→                    // Allow:
 676→                    //     class Foo(
 677→                    //         val bar1: Bar,
 678→                    //         val bar2: Bar,
 679→                    //     ) : FooBar(
 680→                    //             bar1,
 681→                    //             bar2
 682→                    //         ),
 683→                    //         BarFoo1
 684→                    val prevCodeLeaf =
 685→                        startIndentContext(
 686→                            fromAstNode = comma,
 687→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 688→                            childIndent = indentConfig.indent.repeat(2),
 689→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 690→                    startIndentContext(
 691→                        fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 692→                        toAstNode = prevCodeLeaf,
 693→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 694→                }
 695→        } else {
 696→            node
 697→                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 698→                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 699→                    nextToAstNode =
 700→                        startIndentContext(
 701→                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 702→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 703→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 704→                }
 705→        }
 706→
 707→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 708→        startIndentContext(
 709→            fromAstNode = node,
 710→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 711→            childIndent = "",
 712→        )
 713→    }

 724→                        toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 725→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 726→            }
 727→
 728→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 729→        startIndentContext(
 730→            fromAstNode = node,

 732→            childIndent = "",

 736→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 737→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 738→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 739→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 740→            val conditionIndentContext =
 741→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 742→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 743→            startIndentContext(
 744→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 745→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 746→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 747→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 748→            )
 749→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 750→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 751→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 752→        ) {
 753→            startIndentContext(node)
 754→        }
 755→    }

 762→    }

 782→    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {

 870→                it.elementType == ANNOTATION

 957→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 958→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 959→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 960→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 961→                modifierList
 962→                    .children()
 963→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 964→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 965→            }
 966→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 967→            ?: this
 968→    }
 969→
 970→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 971→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 972→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 973→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 974→        ) {
 975→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 976→        }
 977→        return fromAstNode!!
 978→    }
 979→
 980→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 981→        node
 982→            .parents()
 983→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 984→            .lastOrNull()
 985→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
 986→
 987→    private fun currentIndent() =
 988→        indentContextStack
 989→            .peekLast()
 990→            .indent()
 991→
 992→    private fun startIndentContext(
 993→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 994→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 995→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 996→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 997→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 998→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 999→        activated: Boolean = false,
1000→    ): IndentContext =
1001→        IndentContext(
1002→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
1003→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
1004→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,

1138→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {

1156→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)

1163→                    indentConfig
1164→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)

1202→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,

1232→    private data class IndentContext(

1244→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.

1246→        val nodeIndent: String,

1252→        val firstChildIndent: String,

1258→        val childIndent: String,

1311→private class StringTemplateIndenter(
1312→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
1313→    private val indentConfig: IndentConfig,
1314→) {
1315→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1316→        expectedIndent: String,
1317→        node: ASTNode,
1318→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1319→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1320→    ) {
1321→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1322→        node
1323→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1324→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1325→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }

1327→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1328→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1329→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1330→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1331→                    emit(
1332→                        node.startOffset,
1333→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1334→                        false,
1335→                    )
1336→                    return
1337→                }
1338→
1339→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1340→                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
1341→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1342→                    if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()) {
1343→                        // Allow:
1344→                        //   fun foo(): String {
1345→                        //       return """
1346→                        //           some text
1347→                        //           """.trimIndent
1348→                        //   }
1349→                        node
1350→                            .indent(false)
1351→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1352→                    } else if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression()) {
1353→                        // Allow:
1354→                        //   fun foo(
1355→                        //       bar: String
1356→                        //   ) = """
1357→                        //       $bar
1358→                        //       """.trimIndent
1359→                        node
1360→                            .indent(false)
1361→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1362→                    } else if (prevLeaf?.text == "\n") {
1363→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1364→                        // should have no indent as well.
1365→                        ""
1366→                    } else {
1367→                        expectedIndent
1368→                    }
1369→                node.children()
1370→                    .forEach {
1371→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1372→                            (
1373→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1374→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1375→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1376→                            )
1377→                        ) {
1378→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1379→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1380→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1381→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1382→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1383→                                } else {
1384→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1385→                                }
1386→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1387→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1388→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)

1390→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1391→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1392→                                        "string",
1393→                                    true,
1394→                                )
1395→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1396→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1397→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1398→                                    )
1399→                                }
1400→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1401→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1402→                                // the closing quotes.
1403→                                emit(
1404→                                    it.startOffset,
1405→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1406→                                    true,
1407→                                )
1408→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1409→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1410→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1411→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1412→                                        )
1413→                                    } else {
1414→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1415→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1416→                                        )
1417→                                    }
1418→                                }
1419→                            }
1420→                        }
1421→                    }
1422→            }
1423→    }

1435→    /**

1439→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =

1441→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1442→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }

1444→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1445→            .let { indents ->

1447→                    indents
1448→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1449→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1450→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1451→                } else {
1452→                    indents
1453→                }
1454→            }
1455→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }

1479→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1480→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1481→        val nonBlankLines =
1482→            this
1483→                .text
1484→                .split("\n")
1485→                .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1486→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1487→                .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1488→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1489→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1490→            nonBlankLines
1491→                .joinToString(separator = "") {
1492→                    it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1493→                }
1494→                .toCharArray()
1495→                .distinct()
1496→                .count()
1497→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1498→    }

1512→    /**
1513→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1514→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1515→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1516→     * split index.
1517→     */
1518→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1519→        assert(index >= 0)
1520→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex =
1521→            indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1522→                if (it == -1) {
1523→                    this.length
1524→                } else {
1525→                    it
1526→                }
1527→            }
1528→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1529→        return Pair(
1530→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1531→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1532→        )
1533→    }

1535→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {

 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId

 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 9→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
10→
11→/**
12→ * Ensures there are no spaces around unary operators
13→ *
14→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#horizontal-whitespace)
15→ */
16→public class SpacingAroundUnaryOperatorRule : StandardRule("unary-op-spacing") {
17→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
18→        node: ASTNode,
19→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
20→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
21→    ) {
22→        if (node.elementType == ElementType.PREFIX_EXPRESSION ||
23→            node.elementType == ElementType.POSTFIX_EXPRESSION
24→        ) {
25→            val children = node.children().toList()
26→
27→            // ignore: var a = + /* comment */ 1
28→            if (children.any { it.isPartOfComment() }) {
29→                return
30→            }
31→
32→            val whiteSpace =
33→                children
34→                    .firstOrNull { it.isWhiteSpace() }
35→                    ?: return
36→            emit(whiteSpace.startOffset, "Unexpected spacing in ${node.text.replace("\n", "\\n")}", true)
37→
38→            if (autoCorrect) {
39→                node.removeChild(whiteSpace)
40→            }
41→        }
42→    }
43→}
44→
45→public val SPACING_AROUND_UNARY_OPERATOR_RULE_ID: RuleId = SpacingAroundUnaryOperatorRule().ruleId
46→

1132→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent

1143→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1144→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1145→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1146→            SPACE -> {
1147→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1148→                    emit(
1149→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1150→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1151→                        true,
1152→                    )
1153→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1154→                } else {
1155→                    nodeIndent
1156→                }
1157→            }
1158→
1159→            TAB -> {
1160→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1161→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1162→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1163→                    emit(
1164→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1165→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1166→                        true,
1167→                    )
1168→                    indentConfig
1169→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1170→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1171→                } else {
1172→                    nodeIndent
1173→                }
1174→            }
1175→        }

1194→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {

1196→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1197→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1198→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1199→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1200→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1201→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1202→                }
1203→
1204→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1205→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1206→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1207→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1208→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1209→                }
1210→
1211→                else -> ""
1212→            }
1213→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1214→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1215→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1216→        } else {
1217→            ""
1218→        }
1219→    }

1237→    private data class IndentContext(
1238→        /**
1239→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1240→         */
1241→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1242→        /**
1243→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1244→         * on which the indent context starts.
1245→         */
1246→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1247→        /**
1248→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1249→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1250→         */
1251→        val nodeIndent: String,
1252→        /**
1253→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1254→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1255→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1256→         */
1257→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1258→        /**
1259→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1260→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1261→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1262→         */
1263→        val childIndent: String,
1264→        /**
1265→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1266→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1267→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1268→         */
1269→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1270→        /**
1271→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1272→         */
1273→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1274→    ) {
1275→        val nodes: String
1276→            get() =
1277→                fromASTNode
1278→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1279→                    ?.leaves()
1280→                    .orEmpty()
1281→                    .takeWhile {
1282→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1283→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1284→                    }
1285→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1286→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1287→
1288→        fun indent() =
1289→            if (activated) {
1290→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1291→            } else {
1292→                nodeIndent
1293→            }
1294→
1295→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1296→    }
1297→}

1301→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1302→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1303→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1304→            Pair("[", "]")
1305→        } else {
1306→            Pair("", "")
1307→        }
1308→    return prefix
1309→        .plus(
1310→            this
1311→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1312→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1313→        ).plus(suffix)
1314→}

1316→private class StringTemplateIndenter(
1317→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
1318→    private val indentConfig: IndentConfig,
1319→) {
1320→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1321→        expectedIndent: String,
1322→        node: ASTNode,
1323→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1324→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1325→    ) {
1326→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1327→        node
1328→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1329→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1330→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1331→            ?.let { _ ->
1332→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1333→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1334→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1335→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1336→                    emit(
1337→                        node.startOffset,
1338→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1339→                        false,
1340→                    )
1341→                    return
1342→                }
1343→
1344→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1345→                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
1346→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1347→                    if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()) {
1348→                        // Allow:
1349→                        //   fun foo(): String {
1350→                        //       return """
1351→                        //           some text
1352→                        //           """.trimIndent
1353→                        //   }
1354→                        node
1355→                            .indent(false)
1356→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1357→                    } else if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression()) {
1358→                        // Allow:
1359→                        //   fun foo(
1360→                        //       bar: String
1361→                        //   ) = """
1362→                        //       $bar
1363→                        //       """.trimIndent
1364→                        node
1365→                            .indent(false)
1366→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1367→                    } else if (prevLeaf?.text == "\n") {
1368→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1369→                        // should have no indent as well.
1370→                        ""
1371→                    } else {
1372→                        expectedIndent
1373→                    }
1374→                node.children()
1375→                    .forEach {
1376→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1377→                            (
1378→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1379→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1380→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1381→                            )
1382→                        ) {
1383→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1384→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1385→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1386→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1387→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1388→                                } else {
1389→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1390→                                }
1391→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1392→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1393→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1394→                                emit(
1395→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1396→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1397→                                        "string",
1398→                                    true,
1399→                                )
1400→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1401→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1402→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1403→                                    )
1404→                                }
1405→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1406→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1407→                                // the closing quotes.
1408→                                emit(
1409→                                    it.startOffset,
1410→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1411→                                    true,
1412→                                )
1413→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1414→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1415→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1416→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1417→                                        )
1418→                                    } else {
1419→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1420→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1421→                                        )
1422→                                    }
1423→                                }
1424→                            }
1425→                        }
1426→                    }
1427→            }
1428→    }

1430→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression() =
1431→        (prevLeaf()?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevLeaf()?.text == " ") &&
1432→            FUN ==
1433→            prevCodeLeaf()
1434→                .takeIf { it?.elementType == EQ }
1435→                ?.treeParent
1436→                ?.elementType

1438→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock() = RETURN_KEYWORD == prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType

1440→    /**
1441→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1442→     * the closing quotes.
1443→     */
1444→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1445→        children()
1446→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1447→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1448→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1449→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1450→            .let { indents ->
1451→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote =
1452→                    indents
1453→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1454→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1455→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote

1464→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")

1466→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")

1468→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1469→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1470→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }

1472→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1473→        for (child in node.children()) {
1474→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1475→                val v = child.text
1476→                if (v == "\n") {
1477→                    return true
1478→                }
1479→            }
1480→        }
1481→        return false
1482→    }
1483→
1484→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1485→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1486→        val nonBlankLines =
1487→            this
1488→                .text
1489→                .split("\n")
1490→                .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1491→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1492→                .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1493→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1494→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1495→            nonBlankLines
1496→                .joinToString(separator = "") {
1497→                    it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1498→                }
1499→                .toCharArray()
1500→                .distinct()
1501→                .count()
1502→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1503→    }
1504→
1505→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1506→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1507→
1508→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1509→
1510→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =
1511→        elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY || elementType == SHORT_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
1512→
1513→    private fun ASTNode.isClosingQuote() = elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE
1514→
1515→    private fun String.indentLength() = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let { if (it == -1) length else it }
1516→
1517→    /**
1518→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1519→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1520→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1521→     * split index.
1522→     */
1523→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1524→        assert(index >= 0)
1525→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex =
1526→            indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1527→                if (it == -1) {
1528→                    this.length
1529→                } else {
1530→                    it
1531→                }
1532→            }
1533→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1534→        return Pair(
1535→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1536→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1537→        )
1538→    }
1539→
1540→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {
1541→        val firstLeafOnLine = prevLeaf { it.text == "\n" }
1542→        return if (firstLeafOnLine == null) {
1543→            this
1544→        } else {
1545→            firstLeafOnLine.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true) ?: this
1546→        }
1547→    }
1548→
1549→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine(): Boolean {
1550→        var node: ASTNode? = getFirstElementOnSameLine()
1551→        while (node != null && node != this && node.text.isWhitespace()) {
1552→            node = node.nextLeaf()
1553→        }
1554→        return node != this
1555→    }
1556→
1557→    private fun String.isWhitespace() = none { !it.isWhitespace() }
1558→}

251→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
252→                    // Contrary to the IntelliJ IDEA default formatter, do not indent the closing RPAR
253→                    startIndentContext(
